creator: Schiz0phrenic21@gmail.com
text:
I'm getting the error "Bad system call (core dumped)" when I run
"noip2 -C" to create the config file for the noip port that you
maintain.

I csup'ed the ports collection immediately before installing the noip
port, so it is the most recent version.

Also, there is a more recent version of No-IP released on the site,
http://www.no-ip.com/downloads.php?page=linux
The latest version is 2.1.7, while the port is using 2.1.4

I attempted to email the maintainer, kay.abendroth@raxion.net, but the mail daemon said that there is a permanent failure with the message delivery.

How-To-Repeat: Steps to reproduce the problem:
-Install the dns/noip port.
-Run the command "noip2 -C" to create the configuration file.
I then get the error "Bad system call (core dumped)"
